subject: Agricultural Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 3.0. CLIMATE - SMART AGRICULTURE
activities: to climate change and discuss food security and its four dimensions.
resources: Discussing food security and its four dimensions (availability, accessibility, utilisation and stability)
Activity 3: Discussing food security and its four dimensions (availability, accessibility, utilisation and stability)
i. Put learners into small groups of 4-5
ii. Give learners different food samples
assessment: A small-scale farmer in a rural community has planted maize at the beginning of the growing season. However, the area has recently
experienced low and unreliable rainfall, causing the maize seedlings to begin drying up and showing signs of moisture stress. The
farmer is concerned that continued dry conditions may lead to poor crop growth and reduced yields. As a learner who has studied
climate-smart agricultural practices, you are asked to advise the farmer on possible solutions to reduce the negative impact of drought
 Suggest three climate-smart agricultural practices the farmer can adopt to reduce the impact of low rainfall on the maize crop
